title_en: Al-Istidhkar: Ibn Abd al-Barr's Juristic Companion
content_en:
Al-Istidhkar al-Jami li-Madhahib Fuqaha al-Amsar — The Comprehensive Memoir of the Legal Positions of the Jurists of the Major Cities — is the second of Ibn Abd al-Barr's two great commentaries on the Muwatta of Imam Malik, and it serves as a perfect complement to the encyclopedic At-Tamhid. Where At-Tamhid is organized by the names of the narrators in Imam Malik's chains and extends to extraordinary length, Al-Istidhkar is organized by topic in the conventional sequence of Islamic jurisprudence and aims at systematic completeness in its presentation of scholarly positions rather than exhaustive chain analysis.

Abu Umar ibn Abd al-Barr composed Al-Istidhkar as a topically organized digest of the scholars' positions on the legal questions raised by each hadith of the Muwatta. The title Istidhkar relates to the concept of preserving or memorizing scholarly knowledge — the work aims to gather and preserve the positions of the major scholars on every question the Muwatta addresses, creating a comprehensive reference for the comparative legal implications of the foundational Maliki text.

The two works — At-Tamhid and Al-Istidhkar — are designed to be used together, and Ibn Abd al-Barr himself indicated that they were conceived as a pair. At-Tamhid provides the deep chain analysis and biographical context for each tradition, while Al-Istidhkar provides the topically organized legal analysis. Scholars who need to understand the hadith basis of a legal ruling would turn to At-Tamhid; those who need to understand the range of scholarly positions on that ruling would turn to Al-Istidhkar. Together they constitute the most complete engagement with the Muwatta in the classical tradition.

Modern editions of Al-Istidhkar, running to twenty-seven volumes, have made this landmark work more accessible than it was in manuscript form, and digital editions with searchable text have further enhanced its utility for contemporary researchers. It remains, alongside At-Tamhid, one of the most important resources in the hadith and legal sciences produced by Andalusian scholarship.
content_id:
Al-Istidhkar al-Jami li-Madhahib Fuqaha al-Amsar — Memoar Komprehensif tentang Posisi Hukum Para Ahli Fikih di Kota-Kota Besar — adalah yang kedua dari dua syarah besar karya Ibn Abd al-Barr atas Muwatta Imam Malik, dan berfungsi sebagai pelengkap sempurna bagi At-Tamhid yang bersifat ensiklopedis. Berbeda dengan At-Tamhid yang disusun berdasarkan nama-nama perawi dalam sanad Imam Malik dan mencakup pembahasan yang sangat panjang, Al-Istidhkar disusun berdasarkan topik sesuai urutan konvensional fikih Islam dan bertujuan untuk menyajikan posisi-posisi para ulama secara sistematis dan menyeluruh, bukan analisis sanad yang mendalam.

Abu Umar ibn Abd al-Barr menyusun Al-Istidhkar sebagai ringkasan yang terorganisir secara tematik mengenai posisi para ulama terhadap permasalahan hukum yang ditimbulkan oleh setiap hadits dalam Muwatta. Judul Istidhkar berkaitan dengan konsep melestarikan atau menghafal ilmu para ulama — karya ini bertujuan untuk menghimpun dan melestarikan posisi ulama-ulama besar terhadap setiap permasalahan yang dibahas dalam Muwatta, sehingga menjadi referensi komprehensif mengenai implikasi hukum komparatif dari teks Maliki yang mendasar ini.

Kedua karya — At-Tamhid dan Al-Istidhkar — dirancang untuk digunakan bersama, dan Ibn Abd al-Barr sendiri menyatakan bahwa keduanya dikonsep sebagai pasangan. At-Tamhid menyediakan analisis sanad yang mendalam serta konteks biografis untuk setiap riwayat, sementara Al-Istidhkar menyediakan analisis hukum yang tersusun secara tematik. Para ulama yang perlu memahami landasan hadits dari suatu hukum akan merujuk kepada At-Tamhid; sedangkan mereka yang perlu memahami ragam posisi para ulama terhadap hukum tersebut akan merujuk kepada Al-Istidhkar. Keduanya bersama-sama merupakan karya paling lengkap mengenai Muwatta dalam tradisi klasik.

Edisi-edisi modern Al-Istidhkar, yang terdiri dari dua puluh tujuh jilid, telah membuat karya monumental ini lebih mudah diakses dibandingkan ketika masih dalam bentuk manuskrip, dan edisi digital dengan teks yang dapat dicari telah semakin meningkatkan kegunaannya bagi para peneliti masa kini. Karya ini tetap menjadi, bersama At-Tamhid, salah satu sumber terpenting dalam ilmu hadits dan ilmu hukum yang dihasilkan oleh tradisi keilmuan Andalusia.
title_id: Al-Istidhkar: Pendamping Fikih Karya Ibn Abd al-Barr
content_ar: Al-Istidhkar al-Jami li-Madhahib الفقهاء al-Amsar — The Comprehensive Memoir of the Legal Positions of the Jurists of the Major Cities — is the ثانياً of Ibn Abd al-Barr's two great commentaries on the الموطأ of الإمام مالك, and it serves as a perfect complement to the encyclopedic At-Tamhid. Where At-Tamhid is organized by the names of the narrators in الإمام مالك's chains and extends to extraordinary length, Al-Istidhkar is organized by topic in the conventional sequence of Islamic jurisprudence and aims at systematic completeness in its presentation of العلمي positions rather than exhaustive chain analysis. Abu عمر ibn Abd al-Barr composed Al-Istidhkar as a topically organized digest of the العلماء' positions on the legal questions raised by each الحديث of the الموطأ. The title Istidhkar relates to the concept of preserving or memorizing العلمي العلم — the work aims to gather and preserve the positions of the major العلماء on every question the الموطأ addresses, creating a comprehensive reference for the comparative legal implications of the foundational المالكي text. The two works — At-Tamhid and Al-Istidhkar — are designed to be used together, and Ibn Abd al-Barr himself indicated that they were conceived as a pair. At-Tamhid provides the deep chain analysis and biographical context for each التراث, بينما Al-Istidhkar provides the topically organized legal analysis. العلماء who need لفهم the الحديث basis of a legal ruling would turn to At-Tamhid; those who need لفهم the range of العلمي positions on that ruling would turn to Al-Istidhkar. Together they constitute the most complete engagement with the الموطأ in the الكلاسيكي التراث. Modern editions of Al-Istidhkar, running to twenty-seven volumes, have made this landmark work more accessible than it was in manuscript form, and digital editions with searchable text have further enhanced its utility for contemporary researchers. It remains, alongside At-Tamhid, one of the most المهم resources in the الحديث and legal sciences produced by Andalusian scholarship.
